title: Chapter 13: Siege Prep
content: The first thing Marcus learned about siege preparation: it was exactly like raid preparation, except you couldn't look up the boss mechanics on a wiki.

"Entry points," Sera said, pacing the safehouse with a tablet she'd found in the storage vault—pre-loaded with the building's architectural schematics, courtesy of the System. "Six potential breach zones. Ground floor entrance, basement access, fire escape windows on floors one through three, and the roof access hatch."
"The roof? Zombies can climb?"

"Level 2 and above can. They're not shamblers anymore—they're problem-solvers. Give them a wall and twenty minutes, and they'll find a way over it." She tapped the screen. "The titanium walls will hold. It's the *openings* that worry me. Every door, window, and vent is a potential failure point."

Marcus studied the schematics. The safehouse was impressive—titanium shell, reinforced everything, self-sustaining systems—but it was still an apartment building at its core. Apartments had doors. Doors had hinges. Hinges failed.
"System," he said, "fortification options?"

**[AVAILABLE FORTIFICATIONS (unlock via quest progress):]**
**[1. Door Reinforcement Pack — Reinforce all 6 entry points with titanium braces]**
**[Cost: Complete 2 fortification objectives]**
**[2. Perimeter Defense Turrets — Automated kinetic turrets at 4 corners]**
**[Cost: Complete combat milestone with Sera]**
**[3. Frequency Dampener — Weakens Alpha signal within safehouse by 50%]**
**[Cost: Complete intimacy milestone with Elena]**
**[4. Emergency Bunker — Last-resort underground shelter, 72-hour survivability]**
**[Cost: Complete ALL quest objectives]**
Three fortifications plus a bunker. Two required milestones—one combat with Sera, one intimacy with Elena. The System wasn't subtle about pushing him toward connection.

Elena emerged from the medical bay with a notepad—actual paper, because some habits didn't die with the internet. "Medical bay is fully stocked. I've got enough supplies for twenty major traumas or fifty minor ones. Antibiotics, painkillers, sutures, splints, blood bags—I don't know how to use the blood bags, but they're there."
"Can you improvise?"
"I once stitched a guy's hand back together in the back of an Uber with a sewing kit and Super Glue." A ghost of a smile. "I can improvise."

"Good. Because I need you for something else." He showed her the System's fortification list, pointing at item three. "Frequency Dampener. It weakens the Alpha's signal inside the safehouse. Sera needs it—if the Alpha breaks through her resistance again while we're fighting, she goes feral, and we lose our strongest weapon."
"What does it require?"
"An intimacy milestone with you."
Elena's expression flickered—the particular expression of someone recalibrating their expectations rapidly. "Intimacy milestone. Like the hand-holding."

"Like the hand-holding. Or a conversation. Or—" He ran a hand through his hair. "Look, I know this is weird. The whole System is weird. But Sera nearly tore her own skull open this morning fighting the Alpha, and forty-eight hours from now there's going to be two hundred zombies at our door. I'll take weird if it keeps us alive."

Elena studied him for a long moment. Her dark eyes were searching—not for deception, but for something harder to name. Consistency, maybe. Whether the Marcus who'd offered her a choice in the parking garage was the same Marcus asking her to participate in a magic dating system.
"The conversation milestone," she said finally. "What does that involve?"

**[INTIMACY MILESTONE: The Honest Hour]**
**[Objective: Share one genuine secret you have never told anyone with Elena Reyes, and listen to hers in return.]**
**[Duration: Minimum 30 minutes of uninterrupted conversation]**
**[Condition: No distractions. No System interruptions. Just two people being real.]**
**[Reward: Frequency Dampener + Elena Affection +10]**
"Thirty minutes of honest conversation," Marcus relayed. "We share secrets. Real ones."
Elena sat on the arm of the couch. Her injured leg was healing—the fresh bandages and antibiotics doing their work—but she still favored it, protecting the limb.

"I have a lot of secrets," she said quietly. "Most of them are about Derek. And before Derek. And..." She looked away. "I wasn't always with Derek by choice. Not at the beginning. He was kind once. Or I thought he was. By the time I realized he wasn't, I was too scared to leave."
"You're not scared now."
"I'm terrified." She met his eyes. "But I'm more terrified of going back to the version of me who thought she deserved it. So yeah. Thirty minutes. Let's do this."

They moved to the window alcove—a bench seat that overlooked the street below, reinforced glass between them and the dead world. Sera took the living room, giving them space with a respect Marcus hadn't expected from a zombie queen who'd once described territorial proximity as "a biological imperative."
Elena sat with her back to the glass. Marcus sat across from her, their knees almost touching in the small space.
"Who goes first?" she asked.
"Ladies first."

"Chivalry in the apocalypse." She took a breath. "Okay. Secret one: I failed my nursing boards. Twice. I told Derek I passed on the second try because I didn't want him to look at me like I was another disappointment. He already thought I was too soft for med school. I was proving him wrong, except I wasn't proving anything—I was just lying to avoid his contempt."
"Why nursing? If not for him."

"Because my abuela died of something that shouldn't have killed her. An untreated infection. In a hospital. Because no one was paying attention to the old Latina woman in room 304." Her voice was steady, but her hands were clenched in her lap. "I wanted to be the person who pays attention. The person who doesn't let anyone fall through the cracks."
Marcus absorbed this. Elena Reyes, who'd been a victim in Derek's shadow, had originally been driven by advocacy—by the refusal to let someone else be ignored the way her grandmother had been.

"My turn," he said. "Secret one: I've never been in a relationship. Not because I didn't want one—because I was too scared to try. Every girl I liked, I convinced myself she wouldn't be interested. So I never asked, never tried, never risked. I told myself it was because I was focusing on gaming, but the truth is, I was hiding. From rejection. From vulnerability. From the possibility that someone might say yes and I'd have to figure out what to do next."
"Never?"
"Never. You're looking at a twenty-four-year-old virgin whose most intimate relationship was with a guild healer who thought I was a girl because of my character name."
Elena laughed—a real laugh, startled out of her, the kind that made her eyes crinkle and her shoulders shake. "What was the character name?"
"CrystalDove."
She laughed harder, and Marcus felt the absurdity of it—sitting in a titanium fortress, swapping secrets while a zombie horde marched toward them, laughing about MMO character names.

**[INTIMACY MILESTONE: The Honest Hour — IN PROGRESS]**
**[Duration: 0:08 / 0:30]**

"Secret two," Elena said, wiping her eyes. "I knew Gary was dead before Derek told me. I heard Derek push him. I heard Gary scream. And I didn't do anything because I was too scared that Derek would push me next." Her laughter faded. "I've been carrying that for six days. The sound of someone dying because I was a coward."
"You weren't a coward. You were surviving."
"Is there a difference?"
"Sometimes surviving *is* the brave thing. Staying alive when everything tells you to give up—that's not cowardice. That's endurance."

Marcus took his turn. "Secret two: My parents were in Taiwan when the outbreak hit. I've been telling myself they're probably fine—Taiwan had good containment protocols, it's an island, they probably evacuated—but the truth is, I have no idea. The last message I got from my mom was a text that said 'Don't worry about us, be safe.' That was Day 2. I haven't heard anything since Day 3, when the networks went down."
"You haven't tried to find them?"

"Couldn't. Airport's overrun. Highways are parking lots. And I'm not—I wasn't—" He exhaled. "I wasn't brave enough before. The Marcus Chen of Day 1 would have stayed in his apartment and hoped. The Marcus Chen of Day 12 is standing in a titanium fortress with a zombie queen and a nursing student, preparing to fight two hundred dead things, and *still* hasn't found the courage to face the possibility that his parents are gone."
"They might not be," Elena said softly. "You don't know."
"I don't. And not knowing is its own kind of hell."

Elena reached across the space between them and took his hand. Not because the System required it. Not because a quest demanded it. Because he was hurting, and she knew what hurt felt like, and sometimes the only thing you could do for someone in pain was to hold their hand and not let go.
"My abuela used to say something," Elena said. "She said, 'The dark doesn't last. It just feels like it does.'"
"Smart woman."

"The smartest." Elena's grip tightened. "We're going to get through this, Marcus. The horde, the Alpha, all of it. And when we do, we'll find your parents. And my nursing boards won't matter anymore because the whole world is a clinic now, and I'm already qualified for *that*."

**[INTIMACY MILESTONE COMPLETE: The Honest Hour]**
**[Rewards Delivered:]**
**[- Frequency Dampener (Installed — Alpha signal -50% within safehouse)]**
**[- Elena Affection: 32 → 42/100]**
**[- BOND DEEPENED: Marcus and Elena share a foundation of mutual vulnerability]**

**[FORTIFICATION PROGRESS: 3/4 objectives complete]**
**[Remaining: Combat milestone with Sera]**
Three down. One to go.
And then the horde would come, and Marcus would find out if a gamer, a zombie queen, and a nursing student were enough to hold back the end of the world.
